overview.description: AI-powered construction optioneering and scheduling platform for general contractors
overview.description_long: ALICE Technologies builds the world's first construction optioneering platform, enabling general contractors to plan, simulate, and optimize construction projects using AI. Products include ALICE Plan (2D schedule visualization), ALICE Optimize (schedule optimization integrating with Primavera P6 and MS Project), and ALICE Model (BIM-based optimization). The platform helps GCs reduce project duration, improve resource efficiency, and manage risk by running thousands of AI-driven simulations to find optimal construction sequences and timelines.
overview.tagline: Optimize every construction phase with ALICE

history.origin_story: Founded based on AI research conducted by René Morkos during his PhD at Stanford University. The platform addresses chronic construction industry inefficiencies — projects typically run 20% over schedule and 80% over budget.

key_milestones.event: Founded in Menlo Park, CA, based on Stanford University research
Raised fresh capital; total funding reached $76.2M; expanded to 50+ employees
Opened engineering hub in Prague, Czech Republic (Karlín)

leadership.name: Katie Bond

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: ALICE's core value is domain-specific construction optimization algorithms that integrate deeply with GC workflows (P6, MSP, BIM). While AI-native scheduling tools are emerging, the domain expertise, workflow integration, and enterprise sales cycles create meaningful moat — but generative AI planning tools could eventually commoditize the optimization layer.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Construction tech is a large and real market ($1.3T industry) with genuine demand for efficiency, but adoption rates are slow — surveys show ~1/3 of construction employees resist new tech — and the sector has seen high-profile failures (Katerra). ALICE targets enterprise GCs, which limits volume but improves deal size.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Well-funded at $76.2M total raised (as of June 2022) from top-tier VCs including Lightspeed, Foundamental, and JLL Spark, with offices in three countries and 50+ employees. Runway appears solid for continued growth.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: ALICE has real technology, meaningful VC backing, and established enterprise customers, but operates in a conservative industry with slow adoption curves. The risk of AI commoditization over a 3-5 year horizon is real, though its deep workflow integrations and domain specialization provide buffer time.

metadata.notes: Founded year discrepancy: company website states 2015, but a TechCrunch June 2022 article states Morkos 'co-founded Alice in 2013'; using 2015 from official source. Total funding of $76.2M confirmed by TechCrunch (as of June 2022) but may be higher as of 2026. Prague office in Karlín (Rohanské nábř. 678/29) serves as the engineering hub with VP Engineering Jan Dudek based there.
